channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: tech
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: tech

item.title: Rust memory management explained
item.description: Rust shares many concepts with other languages intended for systems programming. For instance, Rust distinguishes between memory allocated from the stack and memory allocated from the heap. It also ensures that variables declared within a scope are unavailable outside of that scope.
item.dc:creator: Info World

item.title: Monday.com aims to be an ‘AI-First’ platform with latest enhancements
item.description: Monday.com has accelerated its push into artificial intelligence (AI) with the announcement this week of its AI vision, which includes three areas of focus: AI Blocks, embedded Product Power-ups, and a forthcoming Digital Workforce of AI agents.
item.dc:creator: Computer World

item.title: The American woman who went viral in Pakistan has a crypto coin
item.description: The “American woman in Pakistan” now has a crypto coin. If you don’t know who that is, American Onijah Andrew Robinson recently went viral after claiming she flew to Pakistan to marry a 19-year-old she met online, only to be rejected.
item.dc:creator: Eve Upton-Clark, Fast Company

item.title: DOGE has disregarded data protection and privacy norms. The consequences will be felt years down the line
item.description: It has been a tumultuous few weeks since Donald Trump took office for the second time as president of the United States, While Trump has garnered headlines for his outlandish executive orders aimed at recasting the country in his own shape, Elon Musk, the tech billionaire turned government quango-bu
item.dc:creator: Chris Stokel-Walker, Fast Company

item.title: Google Calendar users no longer see default entries for events like Pride, Black History Month
item.description: Google’s online calendar has removed default references for a handful of holidays and cultural events — with users noticing that mentions of Pride and Black History Month, as well as other observances, no longer appear in their desktop and mobile applications.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: On average, state cyber leaders are leaving their positions sooner
item.description: The National Association of State CIOs and Deloitte outlined five calls to action in a recent survey of state chief information security officers. The post On average, state cyber leaders are leaving their positions sooner first appeared on Federal News Network.
item.dc:creator: Jason Miller, Federal News Network

item.title: Esports Olympics to debut in Saudi Arabia later than expected in 2027
item.description: LAUSANNE, Switzerland (AP) — The first Olympic Esports Games will be hosted in Riyadh in 2027, two years later than expected when a 12-year deal with Saudi Arabia was signed last year.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, Fox 5

item.title: Samsung Galaxy S26 could get a major battery upgrade that makes it worth waiting for
item.description: A new report claims the Samsung Galaxy S26 could get silicon carbon batteries, making it worth holding fire on grabbing the new Galaxy S25 if you want huge battery capacities.
item.dc:creator: TechRadar

item.title: Ukraine's rare earth elements could help keep US military aid flowing
item.description: KYIV, Ukraine (AP) — Ukraine has offered to strike a deal with U.S. President Donald Trump for continued American military aid in exchange for developing Ukraine’s mineral industry, which could provide a valuable source of the rare earth elements that are essential for many kinds of technology.
item.dc:creator: SAMYA KULLAB, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Streaming is finally profitable. It offers a lesson in patience
item.description: Just a couple of years ago, pundits were warning of streaming’s demise. From Netflix to Spotify, these companies were burning through cash. How could they keep operating? Now, almost all of the streamers have made it to positive profits.
item.dc:creator: Henry Chandonnet, Fast Company

item.title: Apple’s security patch highlights the growing security threat
item.description: Apple’s platforms may be more secure by design than others, but that doesn’t make them invulnerable to attack. That’s why every user should install the company’s latest security patch — it fixes a hole Apple says may already have been in active use.
item.dc:creator: Computer World

item.title: US cracks down on Russian ransomware networks
item.description: The U.S. took aim at two separate Russian ransomware networks Tuesday, announcing new sanctions and arrests against a Russia-based internet service provider and several Russian nationals.
item.dc:creator: Julia Shapero, The Hill

item.title: Russian cybercrime network targeted for sanctions across US, UK and Australia
item.description: The U.S., U.K. and Australia have sanctioned a Russian web-hosting services provider and two Russian men who administer the service in support of Russian ransomware syndicate LockBit.
item.dc:creator: Fatima Hussein, Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: Don't fall for that sneaky iCloud storage alert in your inbox
item.description: Beware of iCloud scams using urgent messages. Tech expert Kurt “CyberGuy" Knutsson helps you spot the red flags to protect yourself.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: The best 360-degree dash cam you can buy just got a big 4K video upgrade and new rear camera support
item.description: 70mai's new Omni 4K Dash Cam is a huge upgrade that delivers 4K 60p video and dual-channel recording with a new rear cam.
item.dc:creator: TechRadar

item.title: Microsoft gets rid of ‘Edge uninstall’ advice page after facing criticism over it having nothing to do with removing the app, and just promoting the browser instead
item.description: Microsoft has made good headway with improving Edge in recent times, but it needs to stop with the promotional activity, particularly the weirder stuff.
item.dc:creator: TechRadar

item.title: Fresh M4 MacBook Air rumors point to an imminent launch, but it could be a modest update
item.description: Apple’s M4 MacBook Air has had its chip identifier number revealed, suggesting its launch is imminent.
item.dc:creator: alexblake.techradar@gmail.com (Alex Blake), TechRadar

item.title: Review: Zencoder has a vision for AI coding
item.description: Zencoder is an AI coding assistant with interfaces to Visual Studio Code and JetBrains IDEs. It is designed to help developers ship products faster, much like GitHub Copilot, Amazon Q Developer, and Tabnine.
item.dc:creator: Info World

item.title: The cloud giants stumble
item.description: Recent financial results from the major cloud providers reveal a concerning trend in cloud adoption and growth. AWS reported just 13% growth in the final quarter of 2023, Microsoft Azure saw 19% growth, and Google Cloud achieved 26% growth—all below market expectations.
item.dc:creator: Info World

item.title: Why the generative AI hype is good
item.description: Elon Musk predicts artificial general intelligence (AGI) by 2025. Former OpenAI employee Leopold Aschenbrenner says AGI by 2027. Futurist Ray Kurzweil bets on 2029 for AI surpassing human intelligence. OpenAI cofounder Ilya Sutskever declared that language models are “slightly conscious.
item.dc:creator: Info World

item.title: Microsoft to rejig Office-Teams cost to avoid EU fine
item.description: Microsoft is reportedly working on increasing the price difference between its Microsoft 365 suite bundled with Teams and the standalone Teams application in a bid to avoid an antitrust fine from the EU.
item.dc:creator: Computer World

item.title: VPN vs. ZTNA: Cisco tackles pros and cons
item.description: It’s no secret that more modern approaches to remote access have been usurping VPNs as organizations adapt to the realities of a distributed workforce, cloud-based applications, and heightened security threats.
item.dc:creator: Network World

item.title: This website combines Wikipedia and TikTok to fight doomscrolling
item.description: “Insane project idea: all of wikipedia on a single, scrollable page,” Patina Systems founder Tyler Angert posted on X earlier this month. “Even better, an infinitely scrolling Wikipedia page based on whatever you are interested in next?” replied Bloomberg Beta VC James Cham. “WikiTok,” added Angert.
item.dc:creator: Eve Upton-Clark, Fast Company

item.title: C++, Go, and Rust gaining popularity – Tiobe
item.description: With Tiobe’s February 2025 rankings, the publisher of the monthly index of programming language popularity notes that fast programming languages such as C++, Go, and Rust are gaining ground.
item.dc:creator: Info World

item.title: News influencers favored Trump over Harris during campaign, study finds
item.description: NEW YORK (AP) — Americans who followed news influencers during the presidential campaign were more likely to hear positive reports about Donald Trump than they were about Kamala Harris, a study has revealed.
item.dc:creator: DAVID BAUDER, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Cisco launches AI Renewals Agent with Mistral AI
item.description: Cisco rolled out its first of many AI agents codeveloped with partner Mistral AI, a genAI and large language model (LLM) startup.
item.dc:creator: Network World

item.title: Rivian opens sales of plug-in vans once exclusive to Amazon
item.description: If it gains traction, the commercial van could become a bigger source of revenue amid a broader slowdown in consumer EV uptake.
item.dc:creator: Bloomberg, The Mercury News

item.title: Google’s latest genAI shift is a reminder to IT leaders
item.description: Every enterprise CIO knows they cannot — and should not — ever trust a vendor’s policy position. Whether that’s because a vendor might not strictly adhere to its policies or can change policies anytime without notice, it doesn’t matter.
item.dc:creator: Computer World

item.title: How AI could strengthen citizens’ confidence in government
item.description: "There are some very prominent ones that we see every day, things like at the TSA with facial recognition," said Valerie Wirtschafter. The post How AI could strengthen citizens’ confidence in government first appeared on Federal News Network.
item.dc:creator: Tom Temin, Federal News Network

item.title: Tech companies raise more than $27 million to build infrastructure for kids online safety
item.description: A group of leading major technology companies including OpenAI and Discord have raised more than $27 million for a new initiative focused on building open-source tools to boost online safety for kids.
item.dc:creator: Miranda Nazzaro, The Hill

item.title: Ye's X account deleted after antisemitic posts
item.description: Ye, the rapper formerly known as Kanye West, has left the social media platform X after a days-long posting spree that included multiple antisemitic comments.
item.dc:creator: Miranda Nazzaro, The Hill

item.title: Google’s generative AI Toolbox for Databases to help connect agents with databases
item.description: Google’s cloud computing division, Google Cloud, has added a new generative AI Toolbox for Databases to help enterprise developers connect agent-based generative AI applications to its databases.
item.dc:creator: Info World

item.title: When LLMs become influencers
item.description: On Sunday people tuned in to watch the Super Bowl—and lots and lots of ads. Online, however, it’s increasingly the case that AI agents will see and interact with ads. Humans won’t.
item.dc:creator: Info World

item.title: OpenAI looks across US for sites to build its Trump-backed Stargate AI data centers
item.description: OpenAI is scouring the U.S. for sites to build a network of huge data centers to power its artificial intelligence technology, expanding beyond a flagship Texas location and looking across 16 states to accelerate the Stargate project championed by President Donald Trump.
item.dc:creator: MATT O'BRIEN, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: One Tech Tip: How to block your phone from tracking your location
item.description: LONDON (AP) — Smartphones are useful tools for everyday life, but they're privy to nearly everything about you, including all the places you've been — if you let them.
item.dc:creator: KELVIN CHAN, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: DOGE’s ‘unimpeded’ access to classified data poses national, economic security risks
item.description: There is no information on who has been hired under DOGE, the authority under which it operates, or how it vets and monitors its staff. The post DOGE’s ‘unimpeded’ access to classified data poses national, economic security risks first appeared on Federal News Network.
item.dc:creator: Anastasia Obis, Federal News Network

item.title: Fast fashion, laptops and toys are likely to cost more due to US tariffs on Chinese imports
item.description: A sweeping new U.S. tariff on products made in China is expected to increase the prices American consumers pay for a wide array of products, from the ultra-cheap apparel sold on online shopping platforms to toys and electronic devices such as computers and cellphones.
item.dc:creator: HALELUYA HADERO, Associated Press, ABC 4
